summary: Tests cover all main requirements (manifest parsing, license lookup, classification, reporting, error handling, workflow structure) with clear organization. However, rigor is limited by missing edge cases (malformed JSON/TXT, various version specifiers, empty manifests, custom lookup script failures) and boundary conditions. Design has loose output assertions (grep on unstructured text rather than exact format validation), brittleness in coupled act tests, and assumes fixture files exist without showing their definitions. Would catch basic regressions but lacks depth for complex edge cases.
